Reverse.description: When ON, the track plays in reverse direction. When OFF, the track plays normally forward.

1 Shot.description: When ON, the track automatically stops after one complete playback cycle. When OFF, the track loops continuously.

Pan.description: Adjusts the stereo position of the track output. L64 is full left, C is center, R63 is full right.

Play Level.description: Sets the playback volume level for the track. Range is 0-200 where 100 is unity gain.

Start Mode.description: Determines how the track starts recording/playing. Options include IMM (immediate), REC END (after recording ends), PLAY END (after playback ends), and FADE IN.

Stop Mode.description: Determines how the track stops playing. Options include IMM (immediate), LOOP END (at end of current loop), REC END (after recording ends), and FADE OUT.

Dub Mode.description: Sets the overdub recording behavior. Options include OVERDUB (layers new audio), REPLACE (replaces existing audio).

Fx.description: Enables or disables track effects processing. When ON, the assigned track effects are applied to the audio.

Play Mode.description: Determines the track playback behavior. Options include LOOP (continuous looping) and 1SHOT (play once and stop).

Measure.description: Sets the measure count for the track loop. Determines how many beats/measures the loop will contain.

Tempo Sync.description: When ON, the track synchronizes to the master tempo. When OFF, the track plays at its recorded tempo.

Loop Sync.description: Determines how tracks synchronize with each other during loop playback.

Balance.description: Adjusts the balance between dry and effect signal. 0 is full dry, 100 is full effect.

Time.description: Sets the delay time or reverb time parameter. Units and range depend on the specific effect.

Feedback.description: Controls the amount of delayed signal fed back into the effect. Higher values create more repeats.

Rate.description: Controls the modulation rate for time-based effects like chorus, flanger, or tremolo.

Depth.description: Controls the intensity or depth of modulation for effects. Higher values create more pronounced effects.

LO Cut.description: High-pass filter that removes low frequencies below the set point. Helps clean up muddy low end.

Hi Cut.description: Low-pass filter that removes high frequencies above the set point. Helps remove harsh highs.

D.Level.description: Direct Level - controls the volume of the unprocessed (dry) signal in the effect output mix.

E.Level.description: Effect Level - controls the volume of the processed (wet) signal in the effect output mix.

Attack.description: Controls how quickly the effect engages when the input signal crosses the threshold.

Release.description: Controls how quickly the effect disengages when the input signal drops below the threshold.

Decay.description: Controls how quickly the effect level decreases after the initial attack phase.

Sustain.description: Sets the level at which the effect is maintained during the sustain phase.

Pre-Delay.description: Sets the delay time before reverb begins. Creates space between the direct sound and reverb tail.

Density.description: Controls the density of reverb reflections. Higher values create thicker, more diffused reverb.

Threshold.description: Sets the input level threshold for triggering gated effects. Signal below this level is cut off.

Gate Time.description: Controls how long the gate stays open after the signal drops below the threshold.

Type.description: Selects the type or variation of the effect algorithm. Options vary by effect category.

Length.description: Controls the length or duration of beat-based effects like scatter, repeat, or shift.

Shift.description: Controls the timing shift amount for beat shift effects. Positive values shift forward, negative backward.

Flick.description: Controls the intensity of the vinyl flick effect, simulating the sound of touching a spinning record.
